Specifically, it harmonizes and makes mandatory the domestic content requirements under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025, amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Investment Tax Credits (ITC) under section 48E. The Act specifies domestic content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit, making these requirements mandatory for obtaining the ITC. For example, it sets a domestic content percentage of 45% for projects starting construction after June 16, 2025, and before January 1, 2026, and 50% for projects starting construction in 2026.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and introduces new requirements for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X). The bill imposes Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions, including requirements for domestically-produced materials or subcomponents, and disallows credits for projects receiving material assistance from prohibited foreign entities after December 31, 2025.
